Red seaweeds (Rhodophyta) on Singapore shores: photo index

seaweeds text index | photo index Seaweeds

Division Rhodophyta Photo index of red seaweeds on Singapore shores
Sheets, ribbons


Halymenia seaweed Halymenia sp.


Halymenia seaweed Halymenia sp.


Ruffled halymenia
red seaweed Halymenia dilatata


Frilly halymenia
red seaweed Halymenia maculata


Lacy
red seaweed Martensia sp.

10-20cm long. Thin red flat blade with fine branches. On coral rubble. Commonly seen on our Southern shores.

10-20cm across. Flat sheets and look like a red version of Sea lettuce (Ulva sp) with feathered edges. On coral rubble. Sometimes seen on some of our Southern shores. 10-20cm across. Flat sheets and look like a red version of Sea lettuce (Ulva sp) with smooth but ruffled edges. On coral rubble. Commonly seen on our Southern shores. Central blade flat somewhat rectangular 8-10cm long with ruffled edges and short irregular extensions. The surface has little bumps. On coral rubble. Often seen on our Southern shores. A lacy circular blade about 5-8cm across. Beige or green with tinges of lilac. Coral rubble near reefs. Sometimes seen on some of our Southern shores.

serrated strips
